aggregation: Asset_DR(x) = sum_i ( capex_weight_i * DR_i(x) ); subsystems with no hail curve contribute 0 (denominator NOT adjusted)

subsystems.note: mono-Si 3.2mm tempered glass module; only junction boxes/wiring survive at extreme hail (L=0.95)
single-axis tracker (Hayhurst is single_axis_tracking); steel/Al is hail-resilient

uncovered_subsystems_zero_damage.note: no hail curve → 0 DR; ~36% of asset value is hail-immune (inverters, transformers, buried cable, civil, SCADA)

metadata.component_curves: research/HAIL_x_SOLAR.md §3.6/§4.6 — IBHS solar hail testing 2019-2023, IEC 61215-2:2021 / UL 61730, GCube insurance (hail = 54% of solar incurred cost), real events: 2019 Midway (>50mm → 58% modules), 2024 Fighting Jays (65-75mm → ~100% panels)
metadata.capex_weights: docs/aggregation-model.md Default Weights — Solar (NREL ATB 2024 + LBNL + NREL SAM)
metadata.confidence: medium — module curve medium-high (empirical+standard); NREL-sourced weights; tracker curve low (expert-judgment)
metadata.vendored_for_reproducibility: copied from the gitignored infrasure-damage-curves symlink; swap subsystems/weights here to refine
metadata.supersedes: the methodology §12 literature curve (which extrapolated to ~100%) and the model-gpr asset_fallback (L=0.50)
